title: How Confessing First Changes Who You're Hard On
caption:
You'll spot his sin before he's even finished talking. Yours takes a mirror and a shove.

That's the pattern. Fast on the speck in him, slow on the plank in you. Jesus named it centuries before group chats: first take the plank out of your own eye, then you'll see clearly to help your brother with his.

Confession is the mechanism. Say your sin out loud to one man before you say a word about anyone else's, and something in you steps down. The judge loses his seat. That's when correction stops being sharp and starts being useful, gentler with her, patient with your brothers, honest about your own stuff first.

This week, before you correct anyone, confess something real to one man. Not a vague one. The actual thing.

Confess your sin before you name his. That's the order that heals a man.

Save this for the next time you're about to call someone out.

#faith #christianmen #godlymen #manofgod #scripture #confession #matthew7 #christianity #brotherhood #discipleship
theme: He's hard on himself, gentle with others.
